slides.title: Clear skin starts *in your kitchen*.
slides.sub: Topicals are the last 10%. What you eat and how you sleep is the other 90%.

slides.title: Spike less sugar
slides.body: *Blood-sugar spikes* drive breakouts. Lower added sugar before you buy another serum.

slides.title: Watch the dairy
slides.body: For some people *skim milk and whey* trigger acne. Worth a 3-week experiment.

slides.title: Eat the rainbow
slides.body: Colorful produce = *antioxidants* your skin actually uses. Brightness from the inside.

slides.title: Sleep repairs skin
slides.body: Your skin *rebuilds overnight*. Short sleep = dull tone and slow healing.

slides.title: Hydrate for clarity
slides.body: Chronic low water leaves skin *flat and tired*. Sip all day, not all at once.

slides.title: Feed your skin. *Watch it clear.*
slides.body: Vibe Health connects your food, sleep & water to how your skin looks — in one simple score.

caption:
That $90 serum is the last 10%. Your food, sleep and water are the other 90%. 🍳

Spike less sugar, test cutting dairy, eat the rainbow, sleep deep. Clear skin is an inside job. 🔖

Vibe connects your food, sleep & water to how your skin looks → link in bio.
youtube_title: Clear Skin Starts in Your Kitchen

bg_prompts.cover: Black-and-white cinematic editorial beauty portrait. Dramatic low-key Rembrandt lighting, deep shadows, dark charcoal background. Glowing dewy skin with fine realistic texture, shallow depth of field, 85mm lens look. Single subject, calm confident mood. No text, no logos, no props. Vertical composition with the subject toward the upper half so the lower third falls into darkness. A woman with flawless clear glowing skin, smooth complexion, soft beauty light on the face.
